channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Floods in Kenya Leave 42 Dead, Nairobi Suffers Most
item.description: Kenya is currently dealing with severe flooding, with the national death toll now at 42. Nairobi has suffered the most, recording 26 fatalities, while the Eastern region has reported 10 deaths. The Ri...

item.title: SACP calls for a 'conference of the Left' including ANC and EFF to address inequality
item.description: The South African Communist Party (SACP) has announced plans to host a 'conference of the Left', inviting the ANC alongside the EFF. This initiative comes amid tensions in the traditional tripartite a...

item.title: Alex Eala expresses support for Coco Gauff's recovery after injury
item.description: Filipino tennis player Alex Eala has expressed her heartfelt support for American tennis star Coco Gauff, wishing her a speedy recovery following a recent injury. Eala described Gauff as an 'amazing c...

item.title: Restoration Work Resumes on Kalhora-Era Tombs in Garhi Yasin
item.description: In Garhi Yasin, Shikarpur district, preservation work has resumed at the historic Thaheeman Ja Quba, a graveyard housing four tombs from the Kalhora dynasty, which had long suffered from neglect, weat...

item.title: PDP Governors Call for Calm Ahead of Appeal Court Decision on Leadership Crisis
item.description: The PDP Governors’ Forum don call on all PDP members and supporters make dem maintain calm as Appeal Court go drop judgment on the party leadership wahala today, Monday, March 9, 2026. Chairman, Senat...

item.title: Gerry Adams faces civil case over alleged IRA bombings in London and Manchester
item.description: A civil case has commenced in London against former Sinn Féin leader Gerry Adams, brought by three men injured in IRA bomb attacks in Britain. The claimants, John Clark, Jonathan Ganesh, and Barry Lay...

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Appointed Iran’s New Supreme Leader
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the late Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei, has been appointed as the new head of Iran’s Islamic Republic following his father’s assassination during US-Israeli strikes on T...

item.title: Rising LPG and ingredient costs may prompt hotels to revise menu prices
item.description: The recent hike in LPG cylinder prices has caused concern in the hospitality sector, particularly among hotels and restaurants in Bengaluru. Domestic LPG cylinders have become costlier by around ₹60, ...

item.title: UK Cancer Mortality Rates Decline Significantly Over the Last Decade
item.description: Cancer death rates in the United Kingdom have reached their lowest recorded level, according to Cancer Research UK. Over the past decade, overall mortality from cancer has decreased by 11%, with 247 o...

item.title: Nigel Farage's Chagos Islands trip involved aircraft linked to Reform UK donor
item.description: Nigel Farage's recent attempt to reach the Chagos Islands military base was conducted on a private jet that appears to be connected to Christopher Harborne, a significant donor to Reform UK. Harborne,...

item.title: Increasing Daily Fiber Intake Supports Gut Health and Overall Wellbeing
item.description: Recent research highlights the importance of dietary fiber for overall health, emphasizing a trend called 'fibermaxxing,' which encourages people to meet or exceed recommended daily fiber intake based...

item.title: Unpaid Domestic Work Hid US Income Inequality Over Decades
item.description: For many years, housework na childcare hid the true picture of inequality in America. Official stats showed men earning more than women, but when you add the value of unpaid work at home, the gap look...

item.title: Three Dead, 19 Injured in Road Crash Near CS Aden Duale's Convoy on Kisumu-Busia Road
item.description: A tragic road accident happened along the Kisumu-Busia Road near Otonglo area, where three people lost their lives and more than 19 others got seriously injured. The crash involved a security vehicle ...

item.title: Cosatu Supports Insourcing Bill to Improve State Services and Protect Workers
item.description: Parliament recently held a crucial hearing on the Insourcing Bill, which Cosatu champions as a necessary step to rebuild state capacity, reduce corruption, and protect workers’ rights. The Bill target...

item.title: US sanctions on Rwanda military officials complicate business relations
item.description: The United States government under Donald Trump don impose sanctions on Rwanda Defence Force and four of dem top military officials. Dis sanctions come after Washington get serious concern over violen...

item.title: Pennsylvania State Trooper Killed While Conducting Traffic Stop in Chester County
item.description: A Pennsylvania state trooper, identified as Timothy O'Connor, was fatally shot on Sunday night while performing a routine traffic stop in West Caln Township, Chester County. The incident occurred shor...

item.title: Accused Bondi mass shooter seeks court protection for family identities
item.description: Naveed Akram, the 24-year-old accused of carrying out the Bondi Beach mass shooting alongside his father Sajid Akram, has applied to the court to suppress the publication of his mother and siblings’ n...

item.title: Iranian Drones Expose Vulnerabilities in U.S. Air Defense Systems
item.description: Iran successfully demonstrated the vulnerabilities of U.S. air defense systems by using two drones in Kuwait, achieving what the U.S. military had not in over a decade. The drones struck two S-band ra...

item.title: Matt Dunstone Claims 2026 Brier Championship with 6-3 Victory Over Kevin Koe
item.description: Manitoba's Matt Dunstone secured the 2026 Tim Hortons Brier, Canada's premier men’s curling championship, with a 6-3 victory over Alberta's Kevin Koe in the final. Dunstone displayed consistent accura...

item.title: Global commodity markets react sharply to escalating Iran conflict with oil surging and gold declining
item.description: The article from Dawn, based on Reuters, describes major disruptions in global commodity markets triggered by the ongoing war involving Iran, the United States, and Israel. On Monday, Brent crude oil ...
